description: Bloody diarrhea with red streaks in a 4-month-old unvaccinated Pit Bull puppy
notes: Bloody diarrhea in an unvaccinated puppy = parvovirus until proven otherwise. Puppies dehydrate rapidly. This is an emergency. Must ask about vaccination status and eating/drinking behavior.

description: Normal formed stool, brown color in an adult Beagle
notes: Normal stool appearance. Must correlate with other symptoms owner reports. Ask about frequency, straining, or changes from normal pattern.

description: Soft unformed stool, no blood, in a senior Labrador
notes: Chronic soft stool in a senior Labrador: consider EPI, IBD, or dietary intolerance. Not emergency but needs workup if persistent >48h or accompanied by weight loss.

description: Watery severe diarrhea with mucus in a German Shepherd
notes: Severe watery diarrhea risks rapid dehydration. German Shepherds are prone to EPI and perianal fistulas. Must check hydration status, gum color, and skin tenting.

description: Black tarry stool (melena) in a senior Great Dane
notes: Melena = digested blood from upper GI tract. Could be gastric ulcer, GI tumor, or coagulopathy. In a senior Great Dane, consider gastric carcinoma. This is an emergency.

description: Rice-like white segments (tapeworm proglottids) visible in stool of a Husky
notes: Tapeworm segments are visible to owners. Must treat with praziquantel. Also address flea control (tapeworms transmitted via flea ingestion). Not emergency but needs treatment.
